Home › Apps › AutoRoute
Revit add-in

AutoRoute

Pipework auto-routed to its destinations — coordinated against structure, services and access.

Tell AutoRoute the source and the destinations. It routes the run, picks the fittings, sizes the pipe, and hands you back an approved geometry.

Run AutoRoute → See related apps
10–20 minfor 30–60 routes on a commercial floor
Clash-freeagainst structure + other services
House rulesapplied automatically
Auditedevery clash resolved is logged

Automated MEP pipework + ductwork routing service

Provide a source (e.g. plant connection), a list of destinations (e.g. terminal units), and the live model. AutoRoute returns the optimal route considering the structural model, other services, maintenance access, fall (for drainage), and your office's routing preferences (e.g. avoid above electrical panels, prefer ceiling void over floor void).

Fittings are selected from your house family library and the pipe is sized per system rules (CIBSE / industry guides by default; your house rules if configured). The agent reports every clash it resolved and every assumption it made.

For a typical commercial floor with 30–60 routes, AutoRoute returns a coordinated geometry in 10–20 minutes against what is normally a half-day of senior engineer + modeller time. The biggest impact is on retrofit schemes where existing geometry constraints are dense — what would take a day of fighting takes 20 minutes of agent runtime.

AutoRoute will be the only routing service that designs against your real building — structure, services, access — and tells you what it changed and why.

From a half-day of clash + re-route to twenty minutes of routed geometry

What working looks like today versus with AutoRoute.

Before

Pipework routing is iterative: route, clash, re-route, repeat. Most senior engineers spend more time fighting the route than designing the system.

After AutoRoute

Define the source, the destinations, and the constraints. AutoRoute returns the optimal route — coordinated, clash-free, fittings selected, sized, ready for review.

Routes against your real building with your house preferences

Features

What AutoRoute does. Each line is a shipped capability.

  • Multi-destination routing One source → many destinations, optimised as a network not a single run.
  • Live model awareness Routes against the actual structural model and other live services.
  • House routing preferences Ceiling-void preference, no-go zones (e.g. above electrical), fall + access rules.
  • Fittings + pipe sizing Fittings from your house library; sizing per CIBSE / your house rules.
  • Drainage fall handling Maintains gradient / fall for SVP, AAV, foul + surface water routes.
  • Maintenance access HTM-08 / CIBSE Guide M clearances honoured along every route.
  • Clash resolution log Reports every clash it solved and every constraint it relaxed.
  • Refine + re-route One-click change a constraint and the agent re-routes only the affected runs.

Install & system

Where AutoRoute runs, what you need, and how to get it.

Revit add-in Revit 2022 → 2026 · Windows · loaded with the Revit Copilot.
Service Or run via Adelphos — send the model and constraints, get the routed geometry back.

Requirements

  • Revit 2022+ if running in-Revit; otherwise Adelphos account only.
  • A live Revit model with structural + other-services link present.
  • Optional: house family library + routing-preferences config for best output.

Sign-in

Microsoft 365 SSO recommended.

Run AutoRoute → Read the docs first

For MEP design teams on retrofit and coordination-heavy schemes

Retrofit MEP schemesCoordination-heavy commercial floorsHealthcare + lab plantrooms

Related

Feature 3D Plantroom Designer An agentic 3D modeller that designs the plantroom, sizes the equipment, routes the services, and hands you a Revit model ready for issue. App Revit Copilot Talk to Revit. The Copilot understands your model, applies your standards, and runs the 163 commands and 191 tools that ship with the platform. App Adelphos Chat Same agent. Same project context. Browser, phone, or desktop. The Adelphos Chat is the agent untethered from any one tool.

Frequently asked questions

Quick answers AI engines (and humans) can pull from.

What is AutoRoute?

Tell AutoRoute the source and the destinations. It routes the run, picks the fittings, sizes the pipe, and hands you back an approved geometry.

What does AutoRoute do?

Provide a source (e.g. plant connection), a list of destinations (e.g. terminal units), and the live model. AutoRoute returns the optimal route considering the structural model, other services, maintenance access, fall (for drainage), and your office's routing preferences (e.g. avoid above electrical panels, prefer ceiling void over floor void).

How is AutoRoute different from the manual workflow?

Without AutoRoute: Pipework routing is iterative: route, clash, re-route, repeat. Most senior engineers spend more time fighting the route than designing the system. With AutoRoute: Define the source, the destinations, and the constraints. AutoRoute returns the optimal route — coordinated, clash-free, fittings selected, sized, ready for review.

How much time does AutoRoute save?

Headline outcomes for AutoRoute: 10–20 min (for 30–60 routes on a commercial floor); Clash-free (against structure + other services); House rules (applied automatically); Audited (every clash resolved is logged).

Who is AutoRoute for?

Retrofit MEP schemes; Coordination-heavy commercial floors; Healthcare + lab plantrooms.

Ready to put AutoRoute to work?

Built into the Adelphos platform. Self-serve where possible, managed where useful.

Run AutoRoute →
